title: OS Command Injection Mitigation - /etc/passwd Leak Attempt

steps.message: Isolate the request logs to identify the vulnerable parameter and source IP for the payload: ; cat /etc/passwd.
Verify vulnerability by injecting a time-delay command (e.g., ping 127.0.0.1) as suggested in KB mitigation step 6 to confirm if input is dynamically executed.
Perform a code review of the application layer. Search for dangerous functions: system(), exec(), shell_exec(), proc_open(), or the backtick operator where unvalidated user input is passed.
Approve the immediate deployment of a WAF rule to block shell metacharacters and the implementation of a code patch.
Configure WAF/Cloudflare to block requests containing shell metacharacters (; | & `) in the identified parameter.

Refactor code to remove direct shell execution. If necessary, use input validation with a strict whitelist of permitted values rather than attempting to strip shell characters which can be bypassed by recursion or encoding.
